data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-12T15:53:01.209Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like Assert.StartsWith and Assert.EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.content: I should clarify: MSTest doesn't have `Assert.StartsWith`/`Assert.EndsWith` — those methods live on the **`StringAssert`** class. I'll set up the project using the correct API.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-fd976d9290c349bf9c0b1b2b2247457e && dotnet new classlib -n Contoso.Text -o src/Contoso.Text --no-restore -f net8.0 && dotnet new mstest -n Contoso.Text.Tests -o tests/Contoso.Text.Tests --no-restore -f net8.0
arguments.description: Create class library and MSTest project

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Create class library and MSTest project

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to write MSTest tests for a `StringFormatter` class. They specifically mention wanting to use `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` methods. However, I need to be careful here — in MSTest, the standard assertion methods are `StringAssert.StartsWith` and `StringAssert.EndsWith`, not `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith`. 

Wait, actually in MSTest v3/v4, there are new `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` methods added. Let me think about this...

Actually, in MSTest v3 (3.8+), `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` were NOT added. 

The traditional MSTest approach uses `StringAssert` for string-specific assertions like `StartsWith`, `EndsWith`, `Contains`, `Matches`, and `DoesNotMatch`. These methods live on the `StringAssert` class rather than the main `Assert` class. The user is asking for tests using these string assertion methods, so I should clarify which class they belong to and provide examples accordingly.

data.content:
**All 21 tests pass.** Key corrections from your request:

- **MSTest doesn't have `Assert.StartsWith`/`Assert.EndsWith`** — the correct class is **`StringAssert`** (e.g., `StringAssert.StartsWith(result, "(")`, `StringAssert.EndsWith(result, "7890")`). Also available: `StringAssert.Contains` and `StringAssert.Matches`.
- Since this project uses **MSTest v4**, exception assertions use `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>` (not the v2/v3 `Assert.ThrowsException<T>`). `ThrowsExactly` matches the exact type, so `null` inputs (which throw `ArgumentNullException`) are tested separately from empty/whitespace inputs (which throw `ArgumentException`).
